competitive_position: SAMI is running a competitive three-vendor shortlist (confirmed 2 March 2026). GEP Worldwide is the strongest current threat: heavy services support (critical for defense complexity and EY partner alignment) and likely proven defense ERP integration capability (SAMI's repeated S/4HANA consignment/MRP questions on 23 Jun suggest benchmarking against a vendor that has delivered this). SAP Ariba is in the mix but SAMI is skeptical (CRM: 'they ask for detail on why not Ariba, clearly they are unliked', likely due to cost/complexity or IBM relationship). Coupa is flagged for strong A&D customers but no call evidence shows Coupa as front-runner. iValua is noted for heavy customisation/localisation fit but not elevated above GEP. Zycus's wedge is the ST Engineering defense reference (13 Jan call: synchronized ERP/procurement go-live, compliance customizations) and AI/Merlin (Next_Step 26 Feb: 'investigating how we can drive a strong AI message'), BUT CRM blockers (15 May) warn 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted', and 23 Jun call shows SAMI asking for non-AI historical price view, suggesting AI is optional or a concern. Zycus must position Merlin as optional, prove defense-grade S/4HANA integration in writing to match GEP, and lean on ST Engineering to win.

champion.source: CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 Champion (15 May 2026); Avoma 13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026
champion.summary: No champion identified. Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor, CRM Economic Buyer) is running an arms-length competitive RFP and has shown no advocacy (CRM 15 May: 'Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role'). The other named SAMI stakeholders (Yousef Alarabi, Ahmed Almania, AhmedKamal) have been present on calls but have asked only functional/technical questions; none has emerged as a coach or advocate. Without a champion, Zycus has no visibility into undocumented decision factors (GEP's pitch, internal politics, the unmapped CFO's preferences) and cannot preempt competitor moves. Developing a champion is the highest-priority action before the 20 July decision.

items.quote: SAMI requires defense-specific controls: lineage for classified materials, trade/FARS compliance, third-party validations and consignment/MRP integration with S/4HANA were raised as open questions to be answered.

items.area: Economic Buyer (financial signatory) above Franck Devienne
items.quote: approval of the negotiated price requires evidence to support it, which adds an additional burden on the buyer
Very arms-length process — Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role.
No quantified savings, cycle time reduction, or compliance risk mitigation stated by buyer on any call.
items.source: Avoma 13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026 (no ROI numbers mentioned)

recommended_moves: Deliver written responses to defense-specific S/4HANA integration questions by 10 July.
Identify and engage the CFO or C-level budget authority above Franck Devienne.
Develop a champion: identify which SAMI stakeholder sees the most value in Zycus and build their advocacy.

Significant effort required torespond with utmost accuracy 25th Mar- RFP extension advised- closing April 21st (EY has agreed to prime and supply 30% local Saudi resources, as required)13th Mar- RFP is now out and under review by team- 2 weeks to seek clarifications and address internal pros/cons2nd Mar Regional military disruption in region- however SAMI have confirmed Zycus is shortlisted (confidentially, to 3) RFP to come out in next 1 weekAll further meetings to be approved by CK in ME26th Feb- Positive presentation/workshop (4 hours). sources: CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 Economic buyer: 'Franck Devienne Senior Procurement Advisor' (15 May 2026)
Avoma 23 Jun 2026 (speaker_id 11): 'approval of the negotiated price requires evidence to support it' (higher authority required)
Contact roles: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor), Yousef Alarabi (Sr. Procurement Specialist), Ahmed Almania (Procurement Specialist) — no CFO/CEO/C-level
Avoma 13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026: Franck present and leading; no senior exec joined
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor, fdevienne@sami.com.sa) is the CRM-identified Economic Buyer (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026) and has been the primary SAMI contact throughout the RFP cycle. Franck led the 13 January 2026 intro call, the 23 June 2026 SRM demo, and all email correspondence (Next_Step__c references 'ongoing clarifications with advisor Franck' on 6 Jun, and 'Franck reached out' on 21 May). CRM states 'Centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' However, on the 23 June 2026 call a SAMI buyer (speaker_id 11) stated 'approval of the negotiated price requires evidence to support it, which adds an additional burden on the buyer' (Avoma objection note), confirming that Franck cannot approve the $2.2M spend alone and a higher authority (CFO, CEO, or C-level sponsor) must sign off. No CFO, CEO, or C-level executive has appeared in any call, contact role, or task. Franck is the operational Economic Buyer (controls the evaluation and vendor selection), but the FINANCIAL Economic Buyer (budget signatory) is unmapped. On a $2.2M defense procurement deal at Shortlisted stage (mid-July decision), the absence of a mapped C-level sponsor is a real commercial risk: the unmapped executive could introduce new commercial conditions, strategic vendor preferences, or reject the pricing post-selection. Zycus must map and engage the CFO or equivalent before final commercials.
